sourceMovie.title: The Faculty
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Faculty for the same reason it broke through in 1998: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a science fiction rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Robert Rodriguez keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The horror and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Alien
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Faculty clicked for you, Alien (1979) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and science fiction, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Faculty?
faq.answer: Try Alien, Nope, Alien: Romulus—each shares DNA with The Faculty while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Faculty?
faq.answer: The Faculty (1998) is best described as Horror, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Faculty worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
